name: Orval (OpenAPI → TypeScript Client Codegen)

auth.notes: Build-time code generation tool; no auth

performance.notes: Build-time code generation only; zero runtime overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Generates fully-typed TypeScript clients from OpenAPI 3.x specs. Unlike openapi-typescript (types only), Orval generates full axios/fetch/react-query/swr/vue-query hooks. Supports: axios, fetch, angular httpClient. Mock generation with faker. Zod schema generation. Watch mode. orval.config.ts for configuration. Major advantage over openapi-generator: TypeScript-native, much better output quality. Used by companies requiring React Query integration from OpenAPI specs. More opinionated but more complete than openapi-typescript.

gotchas: Generated code can be verbose and opinionated
Complex specs may generate imperfect types
More code to maintain (full SDK vs types-only)
best_when: React projects needing react-query hooks from OpenAPI specs
Teams wanting complete client SDK generated (not just types)
Mock generation for testing from OpenAPI specs
Projects migrating from swagger-codegen to TypeScript-native tool
avoid_when: Just need TypeScript types (openapi-typescript is simpler)
Preference for hand-written, minimal HTTP clients
summary: Orval generates complete TypeScript clients from OpenAPI 3.x specs — not just types but full axios/fetch clients and react-query/swr hooks. Mock generation with faker. Zod schema output. TypeScript-native with much better output quality than openapi-generator. Best when teams want auto-generated react-query hooks from OpenAPI specs. Use openapi-typescript instead for types-only without full SDK generation.
